last_substantive_baseline.title: Repair read-only planner RBAC evidence capture
last_substantive_baseline.claim_boundary: This baseline proves the scoped RBAC inventory repair, atomic evidence capture, post-merge CI, OIDC authentication, current Azure inventory, and complete visible RBAC capture. It does not prove completed ARM validation, What-If, deployment permission, report publication, or frontend ingestion.

repository_observation.head_semantics: PR #39 merge commit observed before the ProviderNoRbac planner-repair branch was opened
repository_observation.claim_boundary: This is time-bounded external evidence, not a self-updating claim. Resolve current head, pull requests, and CI from live GitHub whenever the file is read.

authored_change.write_owner: ChatGPT conversation: Azure IaC MSP Lab — bounded ProviderNoRbac planner repair explicitly authorized by Anthony Edgar with 'Proceed'
authored_change.scope: Repair only the read-only report-publication planner's ARM validation and What-If mode after workflow run 29963170574 proved that the Contributor planning identity can inventory Azure and RBAC but cannot pass default Provider validation for a proposed role assignment. Use ProviderNoRbac, preserve no-mutation authority, add regression tests and explicit claim boundaries, and update project handoff records. No Azure permission grant, deployment, role assignment, Storage change, VM command, frontend change, or Azure resource mutation is permitted.
verification_criteria: both az deployment group validate and what-if use --validation-level ProviderNoRbac
planner output explicitly records ProviderNoRbac and that RBAC deployment is not authorized
existing atomic non-empty expected-type JSON evidence controls remain intact
planner remains read-only and contains no Azure mutation commands
bash syntax, project validation, all unit tests, and Bicep lint/build pass for the exact pull-request head
final diff remains limited to the four declared paths
authored_change.failure_behavior: Keep the pull request draft, inspect the exact failing test or CI job, patch only the four permitted paths, and obtain fresh exact-head CI. Do not grant the planner roleAssignments/write or weaken OIDC, protected-environment, exact-commit, confirmation, cost, artifact, or no-mutation controls.
authored_change.rollback: Close the pull request without merge or revert its repository-only commits. No Azure rollback applies because this repair performs no Azure authentication or Azure mutation.

bounded_authority_grants.authorization_source: Explicit user instruction 'Proceed' after the read-only Azure planning boundary was stated
bounded_authority_grants.claim_boundary: This grant authorizes authentication and dispatch only for current-state inventory, ProviderNoRbac ARM validation, and What-If. It does not authorize deployment, role changes, report publication, endpoint configuration, guest commands, or any Azure resource mutation.

resolved_repository_items.claim_boundary: Historical branch only; not repository authority.

proven: exact reviewed merge commit checkout succeeded
bounded authority validation and static read-only tests succeeded
resource group and collector identity observations were captured
resource-group and report-storage RBAC inventories were captured as valid JSON
no report Storage account currently exists
no visible role assignment currently grants the collector report-storage access

latest_observed_planner_attempt.failure: Default Provider validation evaluated the proposed Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments resource and correctly rejected the Contributor planning identity because it lacks Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments/write. The planner must use ProviderNoRbac rather than expanding its permissions.

latest_promoted_evidence.freshness_boundary: This remains the latest successfully completed promoted Azure plan. Run 29963170574 is newer and proves current inventory and RBAC visibility, but failed before ARM validation and What-If; it must not be promoted as a completed plan.

pr39_rbac_capture_repair.claim_boundary: The RBAC-query and atomic-evidence repair worked. The run did not complete ARM validation or What-If because default Provider validation required role-assignment write permission.

authority_defaults.rule: Defaults remain fail-closed. A validated bounded_authority_grants entry may authorize a narrowly scoped exception; no grant may imply Azure mutation authority unless explicitly recorded.

live_state_resolution.repository_head: Query the live GitHub default branch at read time.
live_state_resolution.pull_requests: Query live open pull requests at read time.
live_state_resolution.ci: Query workflow runs and jobs for the exact commit under evaluation.
live_state_resolution.azure: Use fresh authenticated Azure evidence; repository declarations and historical artifacts are insufficient.
